coreIssue: This bi-weekly check-in should test whether Lin’s recent late-night pace reflects temporary launch spillover or a senior-level ownership and delegation problem.
openingQuestion: What is still pulling your time late now that the launches are behind you, and which parts of that work truly need your direct ownership?
listenFor: whether they name a specific ongoing decision, dependency, or stakeholder that is keeping the load high
whether they volunteer concrete work they could hand off versus speaking only in broad responsibility language
if they frame the late nights as a one-off push with a clear end point or as normal for the role
avoid: do not lead with praise that softens the workload question so much that Lin can brush it off again
do not jump straight into solving or reassigning work before you hear how Lin is currently choosing priorities and ownership
goodOutcome: You and Lin have agreed one concrete split between Head of Product work that stays with Lin and one meaningful area of product ownership that should sit elsewhere to keep senior-level pace sustainable.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example of work Lin handled personally after the launches and be ready to ask why it stayed with Lin instead of another product leader or partner.
